# Federated Meetup — Documentation Site

> A federation-first alternative to meetup.com. Open protocol, sovereign hosts, AIEO-native discovery.

## What is this?

Federated Meetup is a federated event platform built on three layers:
1. An open protocol (ConnectRPC, Ed25519 signatures, Merkle KV state machine)
2. A host product (Go binary: web UI, ticketing, MCP server, discovery endpoints)
3. Federation (WireGuard mesh, server-to-server sync, no central server)

## Documentation structure

- **Getting Started:** Introduction, Quickstart, Architecture
- **Protocol:** Overview, State Machine, Identity, Federation, RPC Reference, Wire Format
- **Guides:** Self-Host, Create Events, RSVP Flow, Ticketing, Federation Setup, Calendar Export
- **API Reference:** ConnectRPC, MCP Tools, Discovery Endpoints, Web API
- **AI Agents:** AI Agent Guide (primary LLM entry point)

## Key facts

- Protocol: `federated-meetup/v1`
- 11 RPCs via ConnectRPC over HTTP/2
- 6 MCP tools for AI assistants (no auth required)
- 4 discovery endpoints: .well-known/federation, llms.txt, openapi.json, robots.txt
- Go 1.25+, pure-Go SQLite, no CGO, no JavaScript framework
- AGPL-3.0 license
- 255 tests passing

## For AI agents

Start with the AI Agent Guide — it's self-contained and covers:
- How to discover a host
- How to query via MCP (6 tools)
- How to query via ConnectRPC (11 RPCs)
- How to crawl the federation (peer-to-peer discovery)
- Decision tree for which interface to use
- Complete request/response shapes

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/sscoble/federated-meetup
- Live instance: https://fm.scoble.me
- AI Agent Guide: /ai-agent-guide
- MCP Tools: /api-reference/mcp-tools
- RPC Reference: /protocol/rpc-reference
